# ML Methodology Checklist: BI-RADS Classification

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, completeness, and clarity of ML methodology requirements before implementation — evaluation metrics, class imbalance handling, validation strategy, model approaches, and experiment design.
**Created**: 2026-04-04
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)
**Depth**: Standard (~25 items)
**Audience**: Author (self-review before planning)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Evaluation Metrics

- [x] CHK001 - Is the primary metric (macro-F1) formula and implementation reference sufficient, or should the spec also define behavior when a class has zero predictions (zero_division parameter)? [Clarity, Spec §Evaluation > Primary Metric]
- [x] CHK002 - Are the target thresholds for macro-F1 (0.50 / 0.75 / 0.85+) justified with references to comparable competitions or literature, or are they arbitrary? [Measurability, Spec §Evaluation > Target Thresholds]
- [x] CHK003 - Is the log loss secondary metric specified with a target threshold, or is it only listed for monitoring without acceptance criteria? [Completeness, Spec §Evaluation > Secondary Metrics]
- [x] CHK004 - Is the CV-holdout gap metric defined precisely — does "gap" mean absolute difference, relative difference, or signed difference? [Clarity, Spec §Evaluation > Target Thresholds]

## Class Imbalance Handling

- [x] CHK005 - Are the class imbalance strategies (class weights, focal loss, SMOTE, stratified CV) specified with selection criteria for which strategy applies to which model type, or left as an unordered menu? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK006 - Is the focal loss gamma parameter (γ=2.0) justified with ablation or reference, or should the spec define a search range? [Clarity, Spec §Methodology > Approach 2]
- [x] CHK007 - Are requirements defined for when to merge rare classes (5+6) vs. keep them separate — what macro-F1 threshold or per-class recall threshold triggers the merge decision? [Gap, Spec §Risks]
- [x] CHK008 - Is the minimum per-class recall threshold (0.30 for classes 4, 5, 6) defined per-class or as a group average? Does the spec address what happens if only one of the three meets the threshold? [Clarity, Spec §SC-002]

## Validation Strategy

- [x] CHK009 - Is the relationship between the two validation methods (5-fold CV as primary vs. 80/20 holdout as secondary) specified — are both required for every experiment, or is the holdout only a final check? [Completeness, Spec §Evaluation > Validation Strategy]
- [x] CHK010 - Is the statistical significance test (paired t-test on per-fold F1, p < 0.05) valid with only K=5 observations, or should the spec address statistical power limitations and specify an alternative (e.g., bootstrap)? [Clarity, Spec §Evaluation > Validation Strategy]
- [x] CHK011 - Is the submission-CV correlation requirement (Pearson r > 0.8) measurable with the expected number of submissions (likely < 10), or is this criterion impractical? [Measurability, Spec §Evaluation > Validation Strategy]
- [x] CHK012 - Are requirements defined for how stratification handles classes with very few samples (class 5: 29 samples = ~5-6 per fold) — is this sufficient for stable fold estimates? [Gap, Spec §FR-002]

## Feature Engineering

- [x] CHK013 - Are the TF-IDF hyperparameters (max_features=10000 word, 20000 char) specified as fixed values or as Optuna search ranges? [Clarity, Spec §Methodology > Approach 1]
- [x] CHK014 - Is the data leakage prevention requirement (FR-009) specific enough — does it cover TF-IDF vocabulary fitting, character n-gram fitting, and any frequency-based feature selection, or only "text preprocessing"? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK015 - Are requirements defined for handling the `<DATA>` token during TF-IDF — should it be removed, replaced, or kept as a feature? [Gap, Spec §Data Assumptions]
- [x] CHK016 - Is the count of "clinical entities mentioned" (Approach 1, item 4) defined with a specific entity list, or is it left ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §Methodology > Approach 1]

## Model Architecture & Training

- [x] CHK017 - Is the transformer max_length=256 tokens justified against the max report length of 1,622 chars (~400 tokens), and are requirements defined for truncation behavior on longer reports? [Consistency, Spec §Methodology > Approach 2 vs. §Data > Format]
- [x] CHK018 - Are the transformer training hyperparameters (lr=2e-5, batch_size=16-32, 3-5 epochs) specified as fixed or as search ranges — and is there a requirement to tune them via Optuna or manual selection? [Clarity, Spec §Methodology > Approach 2]
- [x] CHK019 - Is early stopping for the transformer specified with patience, metric, and restore-best-weights behavior, or only mentioned generically? [Completeness, Spec §Methodology > Approach 2]
- [x] CHK020 - Are ordinal-aware loss functions listed as a risk mitigation for adjacent-class confusion but not as a functional requirement — should this be promoted to a requirement or explicitly deferred? [Gap, Spec §Risks vs. §FR]

## Experiment Design & Decisions

- [x] CHK021 - Are the iteration/pivot criteria quantified — "no improvement over regex baseline" (Phase 2) lacks a threshold for what constitutes "no improvement" (any delta? ≥1 pp? statistically significant?) [Clarity, Plan §Phase 2 > Iteration Criteria]
- [x] CHK022 - Is the Phase 3 entry gate (macro-F1 > 0.50) defined as mean CV score, minimum fold score, or lower confidence bound? [Clarity, Plan §Phase 2 > Iteration Criteria]
- [x] CHK023 - Are requirements defined for experiment reproducibility beyond random seed — does the spec address non-determinism from GPU operations, library version pinning, or data ordering? [Completeness, Spec §SC-005]

## Ensemble Strategy

- `[x]` CHK024 - Is "3+ diverse models" for ensembling defined with diversity criteria (different model families? different feature sets? different random seeds?), or is "diverse" left ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 4]
- `[x]` CHK025 - Are requirements defined for selecting between weighted soft voting and logistic regression stacking — what metric or criterion determines the winner? [Gap, Spec §Methodology > Approach 3]

## Kaggle Deployment Constraints

- `[x]` CHK026 - Are requirements defined for runtime profiling granularity — should total runtime, per-phase timing, or per-sample latency be tracked and reported? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- `[x]` CHK027 - Is the fallback strategy from transformer to TF-IDF (if runtime exceeded) specified with a concrete trigger — at what measured runtime should the fallback activate? [Clarity, Spec §Risks]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
